heroTip: Singapore accepts credit cards virtually everywhere — you barely need to carry cash.
bestPaymentMethod: Credit card with low FX fee
bestCard: KBank Visa or Bangkok Bank — broad Visa acceptance across Singapore.
topTips: Credit cards are accepted at almost all hawker centres and shops.
PayNow / PayLah! require a Singapore bank account and aren't suitable for visitors.
Withdraw SGD from DBS / OCBC ATMs in the city — better rates than airport money changers.
Mustafa Centre or People's Park money changers offer some of the best cash exchange rates.
Claim 9% GST refund on purchases of SGD 100+ at the same retailer.
avoidTip: Avoid exchanging money at Changi Airport — rates are lower than city money changers.
dccNote: Singapore has many international retailers — always choose SGD when paying by card.

fxFeeItems.name: FX Fee (Foreign Transaction Fee)
fxFeeItems.detail: Fee your bank charges for foreign card use — typically 1.5%–2.5% of the transaction.

fxFeeItems.name: Spread
fxFeeItems.detail: The gap between mid-market rate and the rate your bank actually gives — usually 0.5%–2%.

fxFeeItems.name: ATM Fee
fxFeeItems.detail: Withdrawal fees from both your home bank and the local ATM — combined can reach $5–$10 per transaction.

fxFeeItems.name: DCC Markup
fxFeeItems.detail: Extra markup when you choose to pay in your home currency — often 3%–10% above the true market rate.

attractions.name: Gardens by the Bay
attractions.detail: Futuristic nature park with towering Supertrees, Cloud Forest waterfall dome, and nightly OCBC Garden Rhapsody light show.
attractions.tip: Cards and contactless payment widely accepted — Singapore is near-cashless. PayNow QR available too.

attractions.name: Marina Bay Sands SkyPark
attractions.detail: Rooftop observation deck spanning three iconic hotel towers at 200m with 360° views of the city and harbour.
attractions.tip: Cards accepted at the ticket counter. The infinity pool is hotel guests only.

attractions.name: Sentosa Island
attractions.detail: Entertainment island with Universal Studios Singapore, SEA Aquarium, cable car, and beach clubs all in one place.
attractions.tip: Cards and mobile pay accepted everywhere — Singapore leads Southeast Asia in cashless adoption.

attractions.name: Clarke Quay
attractions.detail: Vibrant riverside zone with outdoor bars, restaurants, and the G-Max Reverse Bungy adventure ride.
attractions.tip: Cards and PayNow QR codes accepted at all riverside restaurants and bars.

attractions.name: Singapore Botanic Gardens
attractions.detail: UNESCO World Heritage site with 160+ years of history — free to explore, with a National Orchid Garden inside.
attractions.tip: Orchid garden ticket booth accepts cards. Snack kiosks inside prefer cash.
